title: Admin login with valid credentials redirects to Admin Dashboard
description: Verifies that an admin can log in successfully and is redirected to the admin dashboard.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the email input field
Type "admin123" into the password input field

title: Admin login fails with incorrect email and incorrect password
description: Verifies that invalid credentials show an error and the user remains on the login page.

steps.description: Type "wrong-admin@example.com" into the email input field
Type "WrongPassword123!" into the password input field

title: Admin login fails with correct email and incorrect password
description: Verifies that a wrong password for a likely-valid user shows an invalid-credentials error and stays on the login page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the email input field
Type "DefinitelyWrongPassword!" into the password input field

title: Inline validation when email is empty and password is provided
description: Verifies that submitting without an email shows an inline validation error and does not leave the login page.

steps.description: Type "admin123" into the password input field

title: Inline validation when password is empty and email is provided
description: Verifies that submitting without a password shows an inline validation error and does not leave the login page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the email input field

title: Inline validation when both email and password are empty
description: Verifies that submitting with both fields empty shows validation errors and remains on the login page.

title: Login form supports keyboard submission with Enter key
description: Verifies that pressing Enter in the password field submits the form and logs in successfully.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the email input field
Type "admin123" into the password input field

title: Home page shows a list of event cards with key summary fields
description: Verifies that the home page renders a visible list of events and each card shows expected summary information.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is visible
Verify element "event title on a card" is visible
Verify element "event location on a card" is visible
Verify element "event time on a card" is visible

title: Open event details from the first event card
description: Verifies that clicking an event card navigates to its details page and shows full details with a Join button.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is visible
Verify element "event details title" is visible
Verify element "full event description" is visible

title: Event cards show short description preview text
description: Verifies that the home page event cards display a short description snippet to help discovery.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is visible
Verify element "short description text on a card" is visible
Verify element "full event description" is visible

title: Event details page content differs from the card preview (full details present)
description: Verifies that after opening an event, the details page exposes more complete information than the card summary.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is visible
Verify element "event date/time in details" is visible
Verify element "event location in details" is visible

title: Home page handles empty events state gracefully
description: Verifies that when no events are available, the home page shows an empty state message instead of a broken list.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is not visible

title: Home page remains usable when events list is long (scroll to more events)
description: Verifies that users can scroll the home page and still see additional events listed.

steps.description: Verify element "an event card" is visible
Scroll to "the bottom of the events list"
Verify element "an event card near the bottom" is visible
Click on "an event card near the bottom"

title: View event details content on Event Details page
description: Verifies that the Event Details page shows core event information and the join form is available.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Verify element "Full Name" field is visible
Verify element "Email Address" field is visible

title: Join event successfully with valid Full Name and Email Address
description: Verifies a user can join an event and sees a success message and updated registered users count.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Type "Test User" into the "Full Name" field

title: Email validation error when Email Address is left empty
description: Verifies the join form prevents submission when Email Address is missing and shows a validation error.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Type "Test User" into the "Full Name" field

title: Email validation error when Email Address format is invalid
description: Verifies the join form blocks submission for an invalid email format and keeps the user on the Event Details page.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Type "Test User" into the "Full Name" field

title: Join form requires Full Name before submission (client-side validation)
description: Verifies the join form blocks submission when Full Name is missing and shows a validation message.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list

title: User can dismiss success modal/toast after joining
description: Verifies that the success feedback can be dismissed and the page remains usable on Event Details.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Type "Toast Dismiss User" into the "Full Name" field
Click on the "Close" button in the success message

title: Registered users count is present before and after attempting to join
description: Verifies the registered users count is visible on the Event Details page and remains visible after a join attempt.

steps.description: Click on the first visible event in the events list
Type "Count Check User" into the "Full Name" field

title: Admin can access dashboard and see key UI sections
description: Verifies an authenticated admin can load the dashboard and see core dashboard UI sections.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Dashboard shows metrics/overview content after login
description: Verifies that dashboard overview metrics/content are visible to an authenticated admin.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Navigate from Dashboard to Add Event using sidebar link
description: Verifies the Add Event sidebar navigation from the dashboard routes to the Add Event page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Navigate from Dashboard to Manage Events using sidebar link
description: Verifies the Manage Events sidebar navigation from the dashboard routes to the Manage Events page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Navigate from Dashboard to Participants using sidebar link
description: Verifies the Participants sidebar navigation from the dashboard routes to the Participants management page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Logout from dashboard returns to admin login
description: Verifies an authenticated admin can log out from the dashboard and is returned to the admin login page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field

title: Toggle theme from dashboard changes visible theme indicator
description: Verifies the theme toggle on the dashboard causes a visible UI change (text/icon/label).

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Verify element "Theme toggle state indicator" is visible

title: Create and publish a new event successfully and verify it appears in Manage Events
description: Verifies an admin can create a new event with valid data, publish it, see success confirmation, and find it listed in Manage Events.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item
Type "Automation Test Event - Published" into the Title field
Type "This is a test event created by automated UI testing." into the Description field
Type a valid future date and time into the Event Date/Time field
Type "1" into the Minimum Users field
Type "10" into the Maximum Users field
Type "Main Hall" into the Location field
Select a Category option from the Category dropdown
Click the Publish toggle to turn it on
Click the "Manage Events" link or menu item
Verify text "Automation Test Event - Published" is visible

title: Create a new event without publishing and verify it appears in Manage Events
description: Verifies an admin can create a new event while Publish remains off, see success confirmation, and find it listed in Manage Events.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item
Type "Automation Test Event - Unpublished" into the Title field
Type "This event is created with publish turned off." into the Description field
Type a valid future date and time into the Event Date/Time field
Type "2" into the Minimum Users field
Type "20" into the Maximum Users field
Type "Conference Room B" into the Location field
Select a Category option from the Category dropdown
Click the "Manage Events" link or menu item
Verify text "Automation Test Event - Unpublished" is visible

title: Submit with all required fields empty and verify inline validation errors
description: Verifies submitting an empty Add Event form shows validation errors and the admin remains on the Add Event page.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item

title: Validation: Minimum Users greater than Maximum Users blocks submission
description: Verifies the form prevents submission when Minimum Users is greater than Maximum Users and shows an inline validation message.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item
Type "Automation Test Event - Invalid Min/Max" into the Title field
Type "Testing validation when min users exceeds max users." into the Description field
Type a valid future date and time into the Event Date/Time field
Type "50" into the Minimum Users field
Type "10" into the Maximum Users field
Type "Test Location" into the Location field
Select a Category option from the Category dropdown

title: Validation: Event date/time in the past blocks submission
description: Verifies the form prevents submission when an event date/time in the past is entered and shows a visible validation message.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item
Type "Automation Test Event - Past Date" into the Title field
Type "Testing validation for past dates." into the Description field
Type a past date and time into the Event Date/Time field
Type "1" into the Minimum Users field
Type "5" into the Maximum Users field
Type "Old Venue" into the Location field
Select a Category option from the Category dropdown

title: Validation: Non-numeric user limits show errors and prevent submission
description: Verifies the form shows validation errors when non-numeric values are entered for Minimum/Maximum Users and does not submit.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click the "Add Event" link or menu item
Type "Automation Test Event - Non Numeric Limits" into the Title field
Type "Testing numeric field validation." into the Description field
Type a valid future date and time into the Event Date/Time field
Type "abc" into the Minimum Users field
Type "xyz" into the Maximum Users field
Type "Numeric Test Hall" into the Location field
Select a Category option from the Category dropdown

title: Edit an existing event and verify the updated values appear in the events list
description: Verifies an admin can edit an event, save changes, and see confirmation plus updated event details in the list.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation
Verify a table or list of events is visible
Click the "Edit" button for the first event in the list
Fill "Updated Event Title" into the Title field
Verify text "Updated Event Title" is visible

title: Edit an event and cancel without saving
description: Verifies an admin can open the edit UI and cancel/close it without changing the event list.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation
Verify a table or list of events is visible
Click the "Edit" button for the first event in the list
Fill "Title Not To Save" into the Title field
Verify text "Edit Event" is not visible

title: Delete an event successfully and verify it is removed from the list
description: Verifies an admin can delete an event, confirm deletion, and see success notification and removal from the list.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation
Verify a table or list of events is visible
Click the "Delete" button for the first event in the list
Verify the events list is visible

title: Cancel event deletion from the confirmation prompt
description: Verifies an admin can initiate delete but cancel at confirmation so the event remains visible.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation
Verify a table or list of events is visible
Click the "Delete" button for the first event in the list
Verify the events list is visible

title: Validate required fields when saving an edited event
description: Verifies the edit form enforces required fields and prevents saving when required data is missing.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation
Verify a table or list of events is visible
Click the "Edit" button for the first event in the list

title: Verify Manage Events page shows key columns and action controls
description: Verifies the Manage Events list renders expected columns and action buttons for each event.

steps.description: Type "admin123@gmali.com" into the username or email field
Click on "Manage Events" in the admin navigation

title: View participants table for an event with registrations
description: Verifies an admin can open an event’s participants view and see a table with Name, Email, and Joined date columns.

title: Participant table shows Email and Joined date columns
description: Verifies the participants table displays the expected Email and Joined date columns for a populated event.

title: Open participant details by clicking a participant row
description: Verifies an admin can click a participant row and see a participant details view.

steps.description: Click the first participant row in the participants table

title: Switch event selection and see participants update
description: Verifies an admin can select a different event in the participants view and the participant list area updates accordingly.

steps.description: Select a different event from the event selector dropdown

title: Empty state shown for event with no registrations
description: Verifies selecting an event with no participants shows the empty state message.

steps.description: Select an event that has no registrations from the event selector dropdown
Verify text "No participants found" is visible

title: Empty state persists when opening participants view for an empty event
description: Verifies the admin remains on the participants page and sees the empty state for an event with no participants.

steps.description: Select an event that has no registrations from the event selector dropdown
Verify text "No participants found" is visible

title: Error handling when participants fail to load
description: Verifies an error notification is shown and the admin remains on /admin/participants when loading participants fails.

steps.description: Wait for the participants content area to finish loading

title: Participants page renders core UI elements after login
description: Verifies the participants page loads and shows recognizable UI elements indicating the page is ready for interaction.
